author: codex-root

patch.value:
## Epoch ordering

Work newest-to-oldest within stable lineages so current conventions inform, but do not overwrite, historical semantics. Suggested epochs: current release train; post-package architecture; SDK and extension expansion; early CLI and storage foundation; imported or legacy archives.

## Evidence hierarchy

Explicit item IDs and immutable mutation events outrank inferred title similarity. Linked commits, PRs, releases, tests, files, and docs provide provenance. Textual resemblance is only a candidate signal. Canceled experiments and superseded decisions remain visible and are not treated as outstanding work.

## Changelog boundary

Terminal metadata updates can move items into current generated changelog sections. Each bounded batch owns its final tracker mutation, package-owned changelog regeneration and check, diff review, and evidence comment as one atomic handoff.
Wave 2 terminal-history graph backfill: reconstruct closed and canceled lineage in changelog-safe, epoch-ordered batches
Enrich terminal item relationships only after active graph policy is proven, preserving historical meaning and coordinating every mutation with changelog and resolution-evidence governance.

Start only after Wave 1 policy and tooling are accepted and pm-e9zh terminal evidence workflow is unblocked or explicitly coordinated; Partition terminal work by historical epoch, canonical parent lineage, release or incident boundary, and bounded batch size with non-overlapping claims; Infer candidate edges only from durable evidence such as original descriptions, comments, history events, linked commits or PRs, files, docs, tests, releases, and explicit identifiers; Human review is required for ambiguous retyping, inferred prerequisites, conflicting evidence, and high-impact graph hubs; unresolved cases retain uncertainty rather than fabricated links; Corrections append superseding events and preserve prior versions; each item remains restorable and explainable at every snapshot; Every batch includes package-owned changelog generation and review after the final tracker mutation plus duplicate, lifecycle, graph, health, history-chain, and release-boundary validation; Completion classifies every terminal item and demonstrates bounded resumability, stable indexes, and compact audit summaries at million-item scale
Recover decades-scale historical lineage and evidence without rewriting proof, dragging unrelated changelog buckets, or inventing retrospective certainty
Agents and graph algorithms can learn from completed, canceled, superseded, and failed work as first-class institutional context
Enables point-in-time restoration, analogical retrieval, outcome learning, provenance, release archaeology, and trustworthy long-horizon planning
Epoch-ordered terminal graph batches with immutable supersession events, changelog review, uncertainty labels, and complete validation evidence
Terminal items are most of the tracker and carry rich history, but they also present the highest semantic, audit, and changelog risk

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-13: pm-e9zh owns terminal resolution evidence and completed audits inspected history,but no open task owns typed terminal relationship reconstruction in changelog-safe epoch batches; this task is downstream and coordinated,not duplicate.

message: Create Wave 2 terminal-history graph backfill task
